compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: DONE (evidence): Rebar Bot identity ticket 594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d (unsinkable-varying-pelican) created with the ed25519 public key registered (keyring: 1 entry; fingerprint SHA256:PU4nHIqJCRLraIYyRD3XTIAsqOBL9TgTM0EG4e7HFqU). AC1: rebar list --type identity shows it. AC2: with git user.email=joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com, resolve_current_identity() returns 594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d (case-insensitive git-email match). AC3: private key provisioned as SSM SecureString /rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key (get-parameter --with-decryption byte-matches the generated key) AND GitHub Actions secret REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY on navapbc/rebar (gh secret list confirms); added to rebar_secret_params in ssm.tf (IaC); private key generated only outside the repo tree, nothing secret committed. ssm.tf change pushed for review (this commit).

compiled_state.description:
## Bootstrap the shared Rebar Bot signing identity

No identity ticket or SSH authorship key exists yet. Create the one the AWS + GitHub bot
environments will share, and provision its private key into both secret stores.

- Generate an ed25519 keypair for the bot (private key never committed to the repo).
- Create the identity: `rebar identity create --name "Rebar Bot" --email joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com --key "ssh-ed25519 …"` (the `identity` type is gate-exempt, so this bootstrap write is not blocked even under enforcement).
- Declare the SSM parameter in IaC: add `"/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key"` to the
  `rebar_secret_params` list in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` (so Terraform owns the parameter's
  existence + SecureString type, matching every other `/rebar/prod/*` secret; the resource is
  created as a `CHANGEME` placeholder with `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] }`, and the
  real value is populated out-of-band). This is the one code change in this task (→ Gerrit).
- Provision the PRIVATE key VALUE into both secret stores (operator has AWS + GH CLI access):
  - AWS SSM SecureString `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key` (`aws ssm put-parameter --type SecureString`;
    bootstrapped now so the AWS bots can use it — Terraform adopts it on the next apply via the
    `ignore_changes = [value]` lifecycle, `terraform import` if a create-conflict arises, an operator/deploy step).
  - GitHub Actions secret `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` on `navapbc/rebar` (`gh secret set`).
  The per-env consumption wiring (fetch-secrets.sh materialization + container config in 245e; workflow env injection in 9e76/1787) references these already-provisioned secrets.
- Verify TOFU registration and that a `git config user.email = joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` resolves to this identity via the seam's resolver.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A Rebar Bot identity ticket exists with its ed25519 PUBLIC key registered; `rebar list --type identity` (JSON) includes it with the key present.
- [ ] Git-email resolution verified: with the store repo's `git config user.email = joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` and no overriding `.rebar/current_identity` pointer, `python3 -c "from rebar._commands.identity import resolve_current_identity; print(resolve_current_identity())"` returns the Rebar Bot identity ticket id (the resolver does a case-insensitive git-email match against identity tickets).
- [ ] `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key` is added to `rebar_secret_params` in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` so the parameter is IaC-tracked (not created purely out-of-band).
- [ ] The PRIVATE key is provisioned as an SSM SecureString `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key` AND a GitHub Actions secret `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` (both verifiable: `aws ssm get-parameter --name /rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key --with-decryption` succeeds; `gh secret list --repo navapbc/rebar` shows `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY`). Nothing secret is committed to the repo; the private-key file used for provisioning lives only outside the repo tree and is discarded after.
compiled_state.title: Bootstrap the shared Rebar Bot signing identity (ed25519 + identity ticket)
